Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment, and high chloride. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation for underlying metabolic or renal pathology.
